title: ilex buergeri f. subpuberula
intro: Ilex buergeri f. subpuberula, also known as the Japanese holly, is a deciduous shrub native to China and Japan, characterized by its small, white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of compounds like ilicin and aucubin. The health benefits of Ilex buergeri f. subpuberula include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to promote overall well-being.
